Law Passed: CA SJR 9 - Immigrants in California: Mass Immigration Raids
Gemini Response: This law is basically a formal way for California to say they "strongly disagree" with mass immigration raids and the use of the military for enforcement, while promising to put more resources into protecting immigrant families and their legal rights.
Claude Response: This is California's official statement condemning the federal government's mass immigration raids and military deployment in the state, and pledging to protect the rights and safety of immigrant families and workers.

Law Passed: IL HR 622 - Federal Agent Excessive and Deadly Force Condemnation
Gemini Response: The point of this resolution is for the Illinois House to formally condemn recent instances of federal agents using deadly force and to call for an independent investigation into those actions.
Claude Response: This is an Illinois House Resolution that condemns what it calls excessive and deadly force by federal immigration agents (ICE, Border Patrol, etc.) under the Trump administration, calls for an independent investigation into the shootings, and demands the resignation or impeachment of DHS Secretary Kristi Noem.

AZ S 1376 - Department of Emergency and Military Affairs Funding

CA S 852 - Searches: Supervised Persons

IL HR 504 - Fair Judicial System
Gemini Response: The point of Assembly Bill 49 is to make schools "safe havens" by requiring immigration officers to have a judicial warrant before entering private campus areas or accessing student records.
Claude Response: This law requires California schools to protect students from immigration enforcement by not letting immigration agents into non-public areas of school grounds without a court-issued warrant, and not handing over any student or family information to those agents without one either.

Law Passed: IL H 1312 - Illinois Bivens Act
IL H 3247 - School Code
CA A 49 - Schoolsites: Immigration Enforcement
CA S 307 - Public Postsecondary Education: Immigration Enforcement
Gemini Response: This law, SB 307, makes sure that undocumented students at California public universities don't lose their financial aid or academic standing if they miss class due to immigration enforcement actions.
Claude Response: This law requires California State University (and asks UC) to protect undocumented students who miss school due to immigration enforcement — making sure they don't lose their financial aid, in-state tuition, or their spot in class, and can pick up right where they left off when they return.

Law Passed: CA A 419 - Educational Equity: Immigration Enforcement
Gemini Response: Basically, this law makes sure California schools stay a safe place for everyone by keeping immigration enforcement off-campus and ensuring that your personal info or immigration status isn't collected or shared without a very good legal reason.
Claude Response: This law requires California schools to publicly post information telling students and families their rights if immigration enforcement shows up at school.

Law Passed: CA S 580 - Attorney General: Immigration Enforcement Policies
CA A 1261 - Crime: witnesses and informants.
Gemini Response: This law, Assembly Bill 1261, makes it easier and safer for immigrant victims and witnesses of crimes to help law enforcement by protecting their rights and streamlining the process for them to apply for legal status.
Claude Response: This law makes it easier for immigrant crime victims and witnesses to get the paperwork they need to protect their immigration status — regardless of their criminal history, immigration status, or whether the case is still open.

Law Passed: IL H 3326 - AN ACT concerning transportation.
Gemini Response: This law, Public Act 103-0540, basically stops Illinois police and agencies from sharing license plate reader data with other states to help them track or punish people for getting an abortion or for their immigration status.
Claude Response: This Illinois law prevents police from using automated license plate readers (those cameras that scan your car's plates) to track or share your location data with other states or agencies if the goal is to investigate you for getting an abortion or for your immigration status.

Law Passed: AZ H 2862 - General Appropriations Act
Gemini Response: This law is essentially a massive to-do list and piggy bank for the state of Arizona, laying out exactly how much money each government department gets to spend and what specific projects—like school repairs, police equipment, and new technology—they need to finish.
Claude Response: This is Arizona's 2022-2023 general appropriations bill — basically, it's the state's budget law that decides how much money gets spent on various government projects, like moving a data center, building a business web portal, updating school financial reporting, and improving the child care management system.

Law Passed: CA S 836 - Evidence: Immigration Status
Gemini Response: Basically, this law stops lawyers from bringing up someone's immigration status in court unless a judge holds a private meeting first to decide if it's actually relevant to the case.
Claude Response: This law says that no one can bring up a person's immigration status in open court — in either a civil or criminal case — unless a judge privately reviews it first and decides it's actually relevant to the case.

Law Passed: CA A 1259 - Criminal Procedure: Motion to Vacate
Gemini Response: This law, AB 1259, makes it easier for people who are no longer in custody to clear a past conviction from their record if they didn't realize at the time that it would cause them serious immigration problems.
Claude Response: This law makes it easier for immigrants to challenge and potentially throw out old criminal convictions that could get them deported, even if the problem wasn't just with their guilty plea but with the entire conviction or sentence.
